- `createMysqlDb` (function): Opens a MySQL/MariaDB-backed database facade.
- `CreateMysqlDbOptions` (interface): Options for opening a MySQL/MariaDB-backed database facade.
- `createMysqlExecutor` (function): Creates a MySQL SQL executor from an existing executor, pool, client, or URL.
- `createMysqlOrmDriver` (function): Creates an ORM driver backed by a MySQL executor, pool, client, or URL.
- `createMysqlPool` (function): Creates a MySQL pool from a URL using mysql2/promise, imported **lazily** on first use so the npm driver stays a runtime-only dependency (the same discipline as @sisal/libsql's client and @sisal/pg's postgres.js opt-in).
- `insertReturning` (function): Inserts rows and returns them, choosing the best strategy the connected server supports: real INSERT … RETURNING where the facade's detected identity lights it (MariaDB ≥ 10.5), otherwise a transactional fetch-by-key...
- `MARIADB_VARIANT` (variable): The variant value a MariaDB server carries in its {@link https://jsr.io/@sisal/orm | DialectIdentity} — the axis that lets version-gated capabilities (e.g. INSERT … RETURNING on MariaDB 10.5+) light up through...
- `MYSQL_DIALECT` (variable): SQL dialect name used by the MySQL/MariaDB ORM adapter.
- `MysqlClient` (interface): Minimal MySQL client (one pooled connection) surface used by the adapter — structurally compatible with a mysql2/promise PoolConnection. The adapter executes through query() (the text protocol) rather than execute()...
- `MysqlConnectionOptions` (interface): Connection options accepted by MySQL ORM adapter factories.
- `MysqlDatabase` (interface): MySQL/MariaDB-specialized database facade.
- `MysqlDriverKind` (typeAlias): Which underlying driver a @sisal/mysql URL connection uses.
- `MysqlDriverRows` (typeAlias): Row payload of a mysql2 result: an array for reads, a header for writes.
- `MysqlOrmDriverOptions` (interface): Options for creating a MySQL-backed ORM driver.
- `MysqlPool` (interface): Minimal MySQL pool surface used by the adapter (mysql2 Pool-shaped).
- `MysqlQueryResult` (interface): Rows and affected-row count returned by a MySQL executor.
- `MysqlResultHeader` (interface): Affected-row header mysql2 returns for mutations (INSERT/UPDATE/ DELETE), in place of a row array.
- `MysqlServerIdentity` (interface): Parsed (variant, version) half of a MySQL-family dialect identity.
- `MysqlSqlExecutor` (interface): Minimal SQL executor used by the MySQL ORM adapter.
- `MysqlTlsOptions` (interface): TLS settings for a URL-based MySQL/MariaDB connection. Forwarded verbatim to the driver's ssl config (mysql2 and the MariaDB connector accept the same Node TLS fields), so a value here reaches Node's TLS layer...
- `parseMysqlServerVersion` (function): Parses a MySQL-family select version() string into the variant/version half of a DialectIdentity. MariaDB self-identifies in the version string ("11.8.8-MariaDB-ubu2404"); anything else is treated as base MySQL. The...

### @sisal/neon . (packages/neon/mod.ts)

Neon serverless adapter package for Sisal.

- `connect` (function): Convenience alias for {@link createNeonDb}.
- `createNeonClient` (function): Creates and connects a Neon Client using jsr:@neon/serverless.
- `createNeonDb` (function): Opens a Neon-backed PostgreSQL database facade.
- `CreateNeonDbOptions` (interface): Options for opening a Neon-backed database facade.
- `createNeonExecutor` (function): Creates a Neon SQL executor from an existing executor, pool, client, or URL.
- `createNeonMigrator` (function): Creates a Neon migration facade with a database-backed history store.
- `CreateNeonMigratorOptions` (interface): Options for creating a Neon migration facade.
- `createNeonPool` (function): Creates a Neon Pool using the runtime-native Neon driver, imported lazily.
- `DEFAULT_NEON_MIGRATION_TABLE` (variable): Default Neon/PostgreSQL table used to store applied migration history.
- `generatePostgresAddColumn` (function): Generates an ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN statement.
- `generatePostgresColumnDefinition` (function): Renders a single column definition for CREATE TABLE / ADD COLUMN.
- `generatePostgresColumnType` (function): Maps a snapshot column type to a PostgreSQL type expression.
- `generatePostgresCreateTable` (function): Generates a CREATE TABLE statement for one snapshot table.
- `generatePostgresUpStatements` (function): Generates the **non-destructive** PostgreSQL up statements for migrating from → to: CREATE TABLE for new tables and ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN for new columns. Destructive changes (drop table/column, column type changes)...
- `NeonClient` (interface): Minimal Neon client surface used by transactions.
- `NeonClientConfig` (typeAlias): Neon serverless single-client configuration (the driver's ClientConfig).
- `neonClientConfigFromOptions` (function): Extracts a Neon ClientConfig from package connection options.
- `NeonClientConnectionOptions` (interface): Connection options forwarded to Neon Client, with url as an alias.
- `NeonDatabase` (interface): Neon-specialized database facade.
- `NeonDriverQueryResult` (typeAlias): A raw Neon driver query result; rows typed as {@link NeonQueryResultRow}.
- `NeonError` (class): Structured error thrown by Neon compatibility helpers. Extends {@link SisalError} so it inherits credential redaction of the message, preserved cause, and details — no separate redaction path to drift (SEC-011).
- `NeonErrorCode` (typeAlias): Error codes emitted by the Neon compatibility package.
- `NeonExecutorOptions` (interface): Options for creating a Neon SQL executor.
- `NeonMigrateOptions` (typeAlias): Options for applying pending Neon/PostgreSQL migrations.
- `NeonMigrationDefinition` (typeAlias): Neon convenience migration definition with inferred programmatic kind.
- `NeonMigrationInput` (typeAlias): Migration input accepted by the Neon migrator facade.
- `NeonMigrationPlanOptions` (typeAlias): Options for planning Neon/PostgreSQL migrations without executing them.
- `NeonMigrator` (typeAlias): Neon migration facade backed by the PostgreSQL migrator.
- `NeonPool` (interface): Minimal Neon pool surface used by the adapter.
- `NeonPoolConfig` (typeAlias): Neon serverless connection-pool configuration (the driver's PoolConfig).
- `neonPoolConfigFromOptions` (function): Extracts a Neon PoolConfig from package connection options.
- `NeonPoolConnectionOptions` (interface): Connection options forwarded to Neon Pool, with url as an alias.
- `NeonQueryable` (interface): Minimal Neon query surface used by the adapter.
- `NeonQueryResult` (interface): Result shape returned by Neon query-capable handles.
- `NeonQueryResultRow` (typeAlias): One raw result row as returned by the Neon driver.
- `NeonRollbackOptions` (typeAlias): Options for rolling back Neon/PostgreSQL migrations.
- `NeonSqlExecutor` (interface): Minimal SQL executor used by Sisal's Neon compatibility package.
- `NeonSqlResult` (interface): Rows and affected-row count returned by a Neon executor.
- `normalizeNeonResult` (function): Normalizes a Neon query result into Sisal's affected-row shape.
- `pgQualifiedName` (function): Renders the qualified, quoted "schema"."table" name.
- `POSTGRES_DIALECT` (variable): SQL dialect name used by the PostgreSQL ORM adapter.
- `PostgresUpStatements` (interface): Safe (additive) up statements plus the destructive changes that were withheld.
- `quotePgIdent` (function): Quotes a PostgreSQL identifier, escaping embedded double quotes.
- `resolveNeonConnectionString` (function): Resolves url or connectionString into a non-empty connection string.

### @sisal/neon ./ddl (packages/neon/src/ddl.ts)

PostgreSQL DDL helpers for Neon serverless workflows.

- `generatePostgresAddColumn` (function): Generates an ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN statement.
- `generatePostgresColumnDefinition` (function): Renders a single column definition for CREATE TABLE / ADD COLUMN.
- `generatePostgresColumnType` (function): Maps a snapshot column type to a PostgreSQL type expression.
- `generatePostgresCreateTable` (function): Generates a CREATE TABLE statement for one snapshot table.
- `generatePostgresUpStatements` (function): Generates the **non-destructive** PostgreSQL up statements for migrating from → to: CREATE TABLE for new tables and ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN for new columns. Destructive changes (drop table/column, column type changes)...
- `pgQualifiedName` (function): Renders the qualified, quoted "schema"."table" name.
- `PostgresUpStatements` (interface): Safe (additive) up statements plus the destructive changes that were withheld.
- `quotePgIdent` (function): Quotes a PostgreSQL identifier, escaping embedded double quotes.

### @sisal/neon ./migrate (packages/neon/src/migrate/mod.ts)

Neon serverless adapter for @sisal/migrate.

- `createNeonClient` (function): Creates and connects a Neon Client using jsr:@neon/serverless.
- `createNeonExecutor` (function): Creates a Neon SQL executor from an existing executor, pool, client, or URL.
- `createNeonMigrator` (function): Creates a Neon migration facade with a database-backed history store.
- `CreateNeonMigratorOptions` (interface): Options for creating a Neon migration facade.
- `createNeonPool` (function): Creates a Neon Pool using the runtime-native Neon driver, imported lazily.
- `DEFAULT_NEON_MIGRATION_TABLE` (variable): Default Neon/PostgreSQL table used to store applied migration history.
- `NeonClient` (interface): Minimal Neon client surface used by transactions.
- `NeonClientConfig` (typeAlias): Neon serverless single-client configuration (the driver's ClientConfig).
- `neonClientConfigFromOptions` (function): Extracts a Neon ClientConfig from package connection options.
- `NeonClientConnectionOptions` (interface): Connection options forwarded to Neon Client, with url as an alias.
- `NeonDriverQueryResult` (typeAlias): A raw Neon driver query result; rows typed as {@link NeonQueryResultRow}.
- `NeonError` (class): Structured error thrown by Neon compatibility helpers. Extends {@link SisalError} so it inherits credential redaction of the message, preserved cause, and details — no separate redaction path to drift (SEC-011).
- `NeonErrorCode` (typeAlias): Error codes emitted by the Neon compatibility package.
- `NeonExecutorOptions` (interface): Options for creating a Neon SQL executor.
- `NeonMigrateOptions` (typeAlias): Options for applying pending Neon/PostgreSQL migrations.
- `NeonMigrationDefinition` (typeAlias): Neon convenience migration definition with inferred programmatic kind.
- `NeonMigrationInput` (typeAlias): Migration input accepted by the Neon migrator facade.
- `NeonMigrationPlanOptions` (typeAlias): Options for planning Neon/PostgreSQL migrations without executing them.
- `NeonMigrator` (typeAlias): Neon migration facade backed by the PostgreSQL migrator.
- `NeonPool` (interface): Minimal Neon pool surface used by the adapter.
- `NeonPoolConfig` (typeAlias): Neon serverless connection-pool configuration (the driver's PoolConfig).
- `neonPoolConfigFromOptions` (function): Extracts a Neon PoolConfig from package connection options.
- `NeonPoolConnectionOptions` (interface): Connection options forwarded to Neon Pool, with url as an alias.
- `NeonQueryable` (interface): Minimal Neon query surface used by the adapter.
- `NeonQueryResult` (interface): Result shape returned by Neon query-capable handles.
- `NeonQueryResultRow` (typeAlias): One raw result row as returned by the Neon driver.
- `NeonRollbackOptions` (typeAlias): Options for rolling back Neon/PostgreSQL migrations.
- `NeonSqlExecutor` (interface): Minimal SQL executor used by Sisal's Neon compatibility package.
- `NeonSqlResult` (interface): Rows and affected-row count returned by a Neon executor.
- `normalizeNeonResult` (function): Normalizes a Neon query result into Sisal's affected-row shape.
- `resolveNeonConnectionString` (function): Resolves url or connectionString into a non-empty connection string.

### @sisal/neon ./orm (packages/neon/src/orm/mod.ts)

Neon serverless adapter for @sisal/orm.

- `connect` (function): Convenience alias for {@link createNeonDb}.
- `createNeonClient` (function): Creates and connects a Neon Client using jsr:@neon/serverless.
- `createNeonDb` (function): Opens a Neon-backed PostgreSQL database facade.
- `CreateNeonDbOptions` (interface): Options for opening a Neon-backed database facade.
- `createNeonExecutor` (function): Creates a Neon SQL executor from an existing executor, pool, client, or URL.
- `createNeonPool` (function): Creates a Neon Pool using the runtime-native Neon driver, imported lazily.
- `NeonClient` (interface): Minimal Neon client surface used by transactions.
- `NeonClientConfig` (typeAlias): Neon serverless single-client configuration (the driver's ClientConfig).
- `neonClientConfigFromOptions` (function): Extracts a Neon ClientConfig from package connection options.
- `NeonClientConnectionOptions` (interface): Connection options forwarded to Neon Client, with url as an alias.
- `NeonDatabase` (interface): Neon-specialized database facade.
- `NeonDriverQueryResult` (typeAlias): A raw Neon driver query result; rows typed as {@link NeonQueryResultRow}.
- `NeonError` (class): Structured error thrown by Neon compatibility helpers. Extends {@link SisalError} so it inherits credential redaction of the message, preserved cause, and details — no separate redaction path to drift (SEC-011).
- `NeonErrorCode` (typeAlias): Error codes emitted by the Neon compatibility package.
- `NeonExecutorOptions` (interface): Options for creating a Neon SQL executor.
- `NeonPool` (interface): Minimal Neon pool surface used by the adapter.
- `NeonPoolConfig` (typeAlias): Neon serverless connection-pool configuration (the driver's PoolConfig).
- `neonPoolConfigFromOptions` (function): Extracts a Neon PoolConfig from package connection options.
- `NeonPoolConnectionOptions` (interface): Connection options forwarded to Neon Pool, with url as an alias.
- `NeonQueryable` (interface): Minimal Neon query surface used by the adapter.
- `NeonQueryResult` (interface): Result shape returned by Neon query-capable handles.
- `NeonQueryResultRow` (typeAlias): One raw result row as returned by the Neon driver.
- `NeonSqlExecutor` (interface): Minimal SQL executor used by Sisal's Neon compatibility package.
- `NeonSqlResult` (interface): Rows and affected-row count returned by a Neon executor.
- `normalizeNeonResult` (function): Normalizes a Neon query result into Sisal's affected-row shape.
- `POSTGRES_DIALECT` (variable): SQL dialect name used by the PostgreSQL ORM adapter.
- `resolveNeonConnectionString` (function): Resolves url or connectionString into a non-empty connection string.

## Documentation Map

Canonical documentation is linked instead of embedded verbatim so this file stays focused on package boundaries and public API discovery.

- [Sisal](https://github.com/gilvandovieira/sisal/blob/main/README.md) (`README.md`): Sisal is a Deno-first collection of **ORM and query-builder packages**, published to JSR — typed schemas, safe SQL builders, schema snapshots, migration planning, and explicit database adapters — with **lightweight...
- [Changelog](https://github.com/gilvandovieira/sisal/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) (`CHANGELOG.md`): This is the canonical changelog for Sisal workspace releases.
- [API Reference](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/api.html) (`docs/api.md`): This reference is verified against the current workspace package exports and source layout. Sisal is a Deno-first database toolkit, published to JSR, with a driverless core, a driverless ORM facade, adapter-neutral...
- [Migration Notes](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/migration-notes.html) (`docs/migration-notes.md`): Sisal replaces the earlier ORM and migration package direction with a focused database toolkit.
- [Drizzle Parity](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/drizzle-parity.html) (`docs/drizzle-parity.md`): This is the guiding document for how Sisal's API evolves. It maps every relevant part of the Drizzle ORM 0.45.2 surface to Sisal, records where we intentionally diverge, and lays out the roadmap to close the gaps...
- [PostgreSQL compatibility](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/pg-compatibility.html) (`docs/pg-compatibility.md`): Sisal's PostgreSQL adapter (@sisal/pg) is verified end-to-end against a real server on every supported major version. The suite connects with the default driver — postgres.js (npm:postgres) since v0.10 (CF1) —...
- [Neon compatibility](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/neon-compatibility.html) (`docs/neon-compatibility.md`): Sisal's Neon adapter (@sisal/neon) targets Neon serverless PostgreSQL. It speaks the Postgres wire protocol over a WebSocket via jsr:@neon/serverless and **reuses the Postgres SQL dialect and DDL**, so its feature...
- [SQLite compatibility](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/sqlite-compatibility.html) (`docs/sqlite-compatibility.md`): Sisal's SQLite adapter (@sisal/sqlite) is verified end-to-end against a real, embedded SQLite database. The suite opens a temp file with the bundled jsr:@db/sqlite driver, applies generated DDL, and exercises every...
- [libSQL / Turso compatibility](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/libsql-compatibility.html) (`docs/libsql-compatibility.md`): Sisal's libSQL adapter (@sisal/libsql) is verified end-to-end against a real libSQL database. libSQL is a SQLite fork, so the SQL surface matches SQLite; what differs is the **connection** — a local file:, an...
- [Benchmarks](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/benchmarks.html) (`docs/benchmarks.md`): Sisal benchmarks measure the work the **library** does — building SQL, rendering it, and turning driver rows into typed results — in isolation from the database and the network. A query's wall-clock time is dominated...
- [Security](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/security.html) (`docs/security.md`): This is the single source of truth for Sisal's security posture: a **code-level audit**, a **living roadmap** of addressed and open concerns, and the **invariants pinned by tests** so the posture cannot silently...
- [Analytics Readiness](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/analytics-readiness.html) (`docs/analytics-readiness.md`): **Date:** 2026-07-01 · **Status:** A1-A4 🟢 · **Scope:** investigation and type prototype only. No @sisal/analytics package, renderer, runtime, dashboard helper, public export, or generated feature-matrix claim ships...
- [Package Architecture](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/architecture.html) (`docs/architecture.md`): Sisal is, and will remain, a **Deno-first, SQL-first, type-safe** toolkit for **relational** data access. It is not becoming an object-first ORM. As the surface grows from OLTP query building toward ETL and...
- [@sisal/core — the compile-target contract](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/core-ir.html) (`docs/core-ir.md`): The documented, versioned public surface downstream packages (@sisal/etl, @sisal/analytics) compile into — the v0.8 item-3 stamp. What this page declares public is a compatibility commitment; everything else is not.
- [@sisal/core extraction — migration notes](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/core-migration.html) (`docs/core-migration.md`): v0.8 extracted @sisal/orm's lower tier into **@sisal/core** (roadmap item 2): the schema primitives, fragment SQL IR, expression operators, the dialect capability registry, and the dialect-aware renderer. The fluent...
- [Editor LSP Setup](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/editor-lsp.html) (`docs/editor-lsp.md`): Sisal is a Deno workspace, so editors should attach TypeScript files to deno lsp, not to a plain Node/TypeScript language server. A generic LSP client is fine as long as the server process is Deno's language server.
- [ETL pain points](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/etl-pain-points.html) (`docs/etl-pain-points.md`): What actually happens when a @sisal/etl (v0.10 preview) deployment breaks. Every entry is pinned by a live-PostgreSQL scenario in integration/etl_limits_test.ts (run alongside the acceptance suite...
- [ETL scheduling](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/etl-scheduling.html) (`docs/etl-scheduling.md`): Sisal ETL is deliberately **not** a scheduler (v0.10 non-goals). The division of labor:
- [Feature matrix](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/feature-matrix.html) (`docs/feature-matrix.md`): Source of truth: tools/feature_matrix.ts Regenerate: deno task docs:matrix · Verify: deno task docs:matrix:check -->
- [MySQL / MariaDB compatibility](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/mysql-compatibility.html) (`docs/mysql-compatibility.md`): Sisal's MySQL adapter (@sisal/mysql) is verified end-to-end against real servers — **both** MySQL proper and MariaDB, which share one adapter but are distinct capability profiles. Each suite opens a pool with the...
- [MySQL type & DDL mapping](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/mysql-ddl-mapping.html) (`docs/mysql-ddl-mapping.md`): **Date:** 2026-07-01 · **Probe:** perf/mysql_ddl_probe.ts (deno task perf:mysql:ddl) · **Verified against:** MySQL **8.4.10** and MariaDB **11.8.8** (Docker), decoded through the C6-chosen driver (npm:mysql2 with the...
- [MySQL readiness](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/mysql-readiness.html) (`docs/mysql-readiness.md`): **Date:** 2026-07-01 · **Status:** all six C tasks 🟢 · **Verified against:** MySQL **8.4.10** and MariaDB **11.8.8** (Docker), Deno 2.9.0 (+ Node 26 dual-runtime check)
- [Use Sisal from Node](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/node.html) (`docs/node.md`): Sisal is Deno-first, but the same packages are published to **npm** under the **@sisaljs** scope for **Node.js 24+**. The API is identical to the JSR packages — only the install and the scope differ (@sisal/* on JSR...
- [npm Distribution Plan](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/npm-distribution-plan.html) (`docs/npm-distribution-plan.md`): This is the **actionable execution plan** for publishing Sisal to **npm** under the @sisaljs scope, running on **Node.js 24+**, alongside the existing JSR distribution. It is the "how / tasks" companion to...
- [npm Release Readiness](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/npm-release-readiness.html) (`docs/npm-release-readiness.md`): This is a **standing readiness report**, not a scheduled deliverable. It records what must change for Sisal to publish to **npm** alongside JSR and run on **Node.js 24+**, so the work can be picked up cheaply **if...
- [Portability policies](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/portability-policies.html) (`docs/portability-policies.md`): The cross-family value and lifecycle policies Sisal's examples, adapters, and tests follow (v0.8 item 17). Per-engine details live in the docs/*-compatibility.md pages; this page states the policies once.
- [Roadmap Sequencing Audit (v0.6 → v0.11)](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/roadmap-sequencing-audit.html) (`docs/roadmap-sequencing-audit.md`): This is a **second-pass sequencing audit** of the forward plan (docs/roadmap.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/v0.6.0-roadmap.md → docs/v0.11.0-roadmap.md, and the twelve examples/advanced-sql-contracts/). It...
- [Roadmap Overview](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/roadmap.html) (`docs/roadmap.md`): Sisal is a **Deno-first, SQL-first, type-safe** ORM and database toolkit for **relational** data across PostgreSQL, Neon, SQLite, libSQL, and MySQL/MariaDB. It will not become an object-first ORM. This page is the...
- [v0.10.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.10.0-roadmap.html) (`docs/v0.10.0-roadmap.md`): The investigations (v0.6) and substrate (v0.8, v0.9) are done; v0.10 ships the **first preview of Sisal ETL** as a separate package. The model is deliberately small: a **job definition + a single-run runner** that...
- [v0.11.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.11.0-roadmap.html) (`docs/v0.11.0-roadmap.md`): With the analytical IR designed (v0.7) and the core IR stabilized (v0.8), v0.11 ships the first **Postgres-first analytical query layer** — a typed OLAP API that queries rollups and analytical tables using Sisal...
- [v0.4.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.4.0-roadmap.html) (`docs/v0.4.0-roadmap.md`): This document captures the API gaps surfaced while building the examples/neon-hot-feed example — a Reddit-style hot feed on Neon/PostgreSQL that must stay Deno Deploy / Neon-HTTP friendly. Each gap forced the example...
- [v0.5.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.5.0-roadmap.html) (`docs/v0.5.0-roadmap.md`): Where v0.4.0 closed _feature_ gaps surfaced by the neon-hot-feed example, v0.5.0 closes _parity_ gaps **between the adapters**.
- [v0.6.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.6.0-roadmap.html) (`docs/v0.6.0-roadmap.md`): Where v0.5.0 makes the OLTP builder boringly correct across the four dialects, v0.6.0 is the **readiness** milestone: it earns the right to build later packages (and reach a fifth dialect) by proving the foundation...
- [v0.7.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.7.0-roadmap.html) (`docs/v0.7.0-roadmap.md`): v0.7 carries **two parallel workstreams**:
- [v0.8.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.8.0-roadmap.html) (`docs/v0.8.0-roadmap.md`): v0.6 and v0.7 are investigations; v0.8 is where their findings become a **stable substrate**. The goal is to stabilize Sisal's internal SQL representation and expression system — and extract **@sisal/core** — so that...
- [v0.9.0 Roadmap](https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.9.0-roadmap.html) (`docs/v0.9.0-roadmap.md`): With the IR stabilized in v0.8 and **MySQL added in v0.7**, v0.9 hardens the **five** adapters against the ETL/analytics SQL shapes surfaced by v0.6/v0.7, and turns the implicit, scattered dialect knowledge into an...
